result:
All confirmed working (verified with a real build + local server, not just claimed). Here's the summary.

## What I found

This is a SaaS that pivoted from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant) â€” but the migration was left half-finished, which was actively hurting organic traffic:

- **`app/layout.tsx` still shipped the old InboxZap title/meta/OG tags.** Production currently shows the correct Lumina branding, so this repo was one deploy away from silently reverting the live site back to the wrong brand.
- **`/blog` shipped an empty HTML shell** â€” posts were fetched client-side from `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist anywhere in the repo. Crawlers saw zero content; users saw "Could not load posts."
- **`/faq` 404'd** â€” linked in the nav/footer on every page, redirecting to a `#faq` anchor that didn't exist on the homepage.
- **`/features` and `/pricing` had no route in the repo at all**, despite rendering live and being linked from nav â€” deploying this repo would have 404'd both.
- **`/pricing` was missing its canonical tag**; `/features` had two `<h1>`s.
- **All 12 existing blog posts are 100% legacy InboxZap content** (Gmail filters, inbox zero, bulk-delete email) ranking at positions 42â€“58 â€” invisible, and completely off-brand for an AI meeting assistant.

## What I fixed

- Corrected all metadata/OG/schema in `layout.tsx`, added a real FAQ section + `FAQPage` schema, added the missing `/features` and `/pricing` routes (with canonical tags, `Product`/`Offer` schema), rebuilt `/blog` as a server-rendered page with no external API dependency.
- **301-redirected all 12 legacy blog posts to `/blog`** rather than harvesting or leaving them as dead links â€” none were on-topic enough to repurpose, but all held enough impressions (per GSC) to be worth preserving equity for, per the harvest/redirect/sunset framework. Full per-URL rationale is in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md`.
- Started the first forward-looking content cluster (`ai-meeting-assistant`): published a pillar article and one long-tail piece, with 4 more planned in `.seoagent/roadmap.md`.
- Verified everything with a real `npm run build` + local server: all routes return 200, redirects return 308 to the right targets, JSON-LD renders correctly.

All findings, the migration plan, and the content roadmap are persisted in `.seoagent/` for continuity. Next up per the roadmap: finish the remaining 4 articles in the `ai-meeting-assistant` cluster, and connect Google Search Console for real-time data instead of the static CSV export used this session.

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:52272. Source repo is the current working directory. Google Search Console export is in ./gsc/.
